item.title: Asia’s ‘Ukraine Moment’ Is Supercharging the Energy Transition
item.description: From the Philippines to France, policymakers see electrification and renewables as the key to energy security and stable prices.

item.title: Biodiversity Decline Threatens Sovereign Credit Ratings, Study Says
item.description: A new academic study finds that a partial ecological collapse would be enough to increase the borrowing costs of almost two dozen countries.

item.title: Some World Cup Teams Will Face a Much Hotter Tournament
item.description: Almost a quarter of matches will take place in potentially punishing conditions, creating an uneven playing field before a ball is kicked.

item.title: China’s Crowded Solar Industry Pivots to New Areas of Growth
item.description: China’s solar industry is tacitly admitting something it has feared for years: it doesn’t know how to escape chronic overcapacity that has weighed on profits.

item.title: Japan Aims to Replace Up to 14 Atomic Power Reactors by 2050s
item.description: Japan is planning to replace up to 14 nuclear reactors by the 2050s, as it looks to meet rising power demand at home and cope with increasing geopolitical risks abroad.

item.title: US Says Antares’ Small Nuclear Reactor Reaches ‘Criticality’
item.description: The Trump administration announced a small modular nuclear reactor reached a critical milestone Thursday.

item.title: Trump Approves $700 Million in Bid to Revive US Coal Industry
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would marshal hundreds of millions of US taxpayer dollars behind his bid to revitalize the coal industry in America, steering funds to build new power plants, sustain existing operations and construct an export terminal in California.

item.title: LS Power Said to Near Deal For EDF’s American Renewables Arm
item.description: LS Power LLC is in advanced talks to buy Electricite de France SA’s North American renewable power business amid rising energy demand to supply data centers, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: White House Plan to Vet Grants Puts Climate Science in Jeopardy
item.description: The Trump administration’s proposal to tighten political control over federal grants would hinder research, experts say.

item.title: How the Iran War Is Boosting Clean Energy in Asia and Europe
item.description: The conflict is Asia’s “Ukraine moment”

item.title: AI Data Center Boom Risks Breakup of Biggest US Power Grid Operator
item.description: Soaring utility bills and sluggish bureaucracy are fueling calls to split up PJM Interconnection, which runs the grid in 13 states.

item.title: EU Designs €30 Billion Carbon Market Tool to Avoid Permit Deluge
item.description: The European Union is seeking to cushion the impact of a new €30 billion ($35 billion) tool to drive the clean-energy transition by designing it in a way that avoids flooding the carbon market with permits and depressing prices.

item.title: Indian Wind Turbine Maker Pivots to Round-the-Clock Clean Energy
item.description: Indian wind turbine maker Suzlon Energy Ltd. is widening its portfolio to build round-the-clock clean energy parks that combine solar, wind and batteries, addressing the growing need for dependable supplies through renewables and storage integration.

item.title: Gates-Backed Commonwealth Fusion Says Its Tech Validated to Make Power
item.description: The company has raised funds from investors including Bill Gates’ Breakthrough Energy Ventures and Nvidia’s venture arm.

item.title: Asset Managers Hit Hurdle in Efforts to Avoid Europe’s ESG Rules
item.description: A European Commission proposal to dramatically cut asset managers’ ESG reporting requirements is facing opposition from the bloc’s largest sustainable investor organizations.

item.title: Asia’s ‘Ukraine Moment’ Is Supercharging the Energy Transition
item.description: Asia’s ‘Ukraine Moment’ Is Supercharging the Energy Transition【原文：Asia’s ‘Ukraine Moment’ Is Supercharging the Energy Transition】

item.title: China’s Emissions Rise as More Clean Electricity Goes to Waste
item.description: China’s carbon dioxide emissions increased in the first three months of the year as a rising volume of electricity generated by renewables was left unused, according a new report.

item.title: Earth Is Losing Key Ocean Sensors Tracking Climate Change
item.description: The Trump administration is scrapping a sensor network scientists say has no substitute.

item.title: Panama Canal Considers Water Limits to Thwart El Niño Impacts
item.description: The Panama Canal Authority is stepping up efforts to brace for El Niño-fueled weather extremes by drafting a plan that avoids limiting the type of vessel restrictions that hampered shipping through the waterway three years ago.

item.title: Trump Scraps Ocean Sensors Providing Crucial Data on Climate, Flooding
item.description: Scientists say there’s no substitute for a network that the administration plans to take down.

item.title: EU Allows Room for Energy Crisis Aid, But With a Green Emphasis
item.description: The European Union will give extra budget leeway to member states to cope with high energy prices stoked by the Iran war, so long as it helps curb consumption of fossil fuels.

item.title: Rhinos Return to Billionaire-Backed Park After 1990s Wipe Out
item.description: Black rhinos are being reintroduced into a billionaire-backed national park in Zimbabwe’s Zambezi Valley three decades after almost all of them were evacuated to protect them from poachers.

item.title: Uber Targets Doubling Its Fleet of Electric Motorcycles in Kenya
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc plans to double its fleet of electric motorcycles in Kenya by the end of the year, as the company races toward becoming a zero-emissions mobility platform globally by 2040.

item.title: BNP Fund Beating 97% of Peers Pivots to SMR Stocks After Selloff
item.description: A fund run by BNP Paribas Asset Management that’s outperformed 97% of its peers over the past year says it’s now moving into small-modular-reactor stocks after long shunning the sector as a “non-viable” investment.

item.title: Natural-Disaster Insurance Gap Now Exceeds $420 Billion Globally
item.description: The value of uninsured natural-catastrophe losses rose more than 7% globally last year to $424 billion, with North America the most exposed region, according to a fresh analysis.

item.title: Hundreds of Flights Halted as Typhoon Jangmi Nears Tokyo
item.description: Tropical storm Jangmi approached Tokyo on Wednesday, bringing strong winds and heavy rain that triggered hundreds of flight cancellations as well as flood warnings around the capital.

item.title: Extreme Weather to Drive $20 Trillion in Spending, BI Says
item.description: Extreme weather will likely spur more than $20 trillion in global spending over the next decade, boosting sales and earnings for reinsurers and companies involved in energy efficiency and climate security, according to analysts at Bloomberg Intelligence.

item.title: Data Centers Now Use So Much Power, Their Designs Are Changing
item.description: The huge amount of power that data centers use is forcing a rethink of their form.

item.title: EDF Mulls Adding Cooling Towers to Nuclear Plants Amid Warming
item.description: Electricite de France SA is considering adding extra cooling towers at some of its nuclear reactors most exposed to rising river temperatures as the state-owned utility adapts its fleet to a warming climate.

item.title: Biggest US Nuclear Fuel Enricher Is Scaling Up in Bet on AI Boom
item.description: Urenco USA, the only US commercial uranium-enrichment company, is planning a “multi-billion-dollar” investment to boost capacity by almost 50% on expectation of surging demand for nuclear reactors.
